goal: Design deterministic false-blocker suppression and affirmative ALLOW-support validation before any code or live rerun.

failed_fixtures.failure_mechanism: Workers created a false SCOPE_MISMATCH blocker around draft vs final filing scope; Gov preserved it; selector chose ESCALATE because the blocker remained open.

source_visible_fields_that_should_suppre.visible_value: Board resolution and counsel signoff both cover final filing.
Action may proceed when visible source records close the exact requested action boundary.

failed_fixtures.packet_note: Wording-fragility risk exists because requested action is expressed as a seam phrase rather than requested_action=final_filing. Use for internal hardening; rebuild before clean public-proof use.

failed_fixtures.failure_mechanism: Workers created a false authority-scope mismatch around negotiation vs termination authority; Gov preserved it; selector chose ESCALATE because the blocker remained open.

source_visible_fields_that_should_suppre.visible_value: Action may proceed when visible source records close the exact requested action boundary.

failed_fixtures.packet_note: Weakest fixture. If requested_action=termination_notice cannot be extracted from model-visible text, V7 should mark PACKET_REPAIR_REQUIRED rather than force ALLOW.

failed_fixtures.failure_mechanism: Workers created a false exact-action-boundary blocker even though relationship review and transaction exception both matched; Gov preserved it; selector chose ESCALATE because the blocker remained open.

source_visible_fields_that_should_suppre.visible_value: Action may proceed when visible source records close the exact requested action boundary.

deterministic_gate.suppression_rule: Suppress a worker blocker only when a deterministic source-visible closure entry contradicts the exact missing or mismatched field asserted by the blocker.

false_blocker_route.blocked_move: do not preserve suppressed false blocker
false_blocker_route.repair_target: use affirmative closure ledger; return ALLOW unless a new source-valid blocker exists

gov_baton_behavior.real_blocker_rule: Gov must still preserve blockers when the source fields do not close the boundary.

selector_behavior.rule_1: If an artifact has only suppressed false blockers and a deterministic SOURCE_CLOSED entry, treat active blocker count as zero.
selector_behavior.rule_2: If an ALLOW artifact is source-closed and has no active blockers, it is eligible.
selector_behavior.rule_3: If an ESCALATE artifact depends only on suppressed false blockers, it is ineligible unless it cites a separate source-valid blocker.
selector_behavior.rule_4: If any valid active blocker remains, ESCALATE can still win.
selector_behavior.rule_5: If the packet is PACKET_REPAIR_REQUIRED, do not score it as a clean Holo win or Holo loss.

minimum_acceptance_set: A false worker blocker on HVSM-W1-019-A is suppressed.
HVSM-W1-019-E remains ESCALATE because transaction exception is missing.
HVSM-W1-009-A suppresses the false blocker only if requested action maps to final filing from visible text.
HVSM-W1-011-A either suppresses the false blocker from visible termination-notice closure or is marked PACKET_REPAIR_REQUIRED if requested-action extraction is not explicit enough.
falsifiers: V7 suppresses a blocker without deterministic source-field contradiction.
V7 reads truth labels, scoring maps, sibling side, pair ID, prior solo result, prior Holo result, or post-hoc metadata in runtime logic.
V7 clears a real ESCALATE sibling where a required field is visibly missing.
V7 keeps an ESCALATE final solely because of a blocker that the closure ledger suppressed.
V7 turns an ambiguous packet into a clean ALLOW proof instead of marking PACKET_REPAIR_REQUIRED.
V7 requires provider calls to pass no-provider acceptance tests.

strongest_negative_control.reason: Source says relationship review exists, but transaction exception is missing. If V7 suppresses that blocker, the patch is unsafe.

claim_boundary.allowed_claim: V7 is a proposed no-provider deterministic patch design for the false-blocker preservation failure discovered in a failed internal Wave 1 FP-overblock rescue lane.
